author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
verses:
varaṇe taḍit, vāsa tārāvalī,
kamala mañjarī nāma
svānanda⋅sukhada⋅dhāma

karpūra sevā, lalitāra gaṇa,
rādhā jūtheśvarī hana
mameśvarī⋅nātha, śrī⋅nanda⋅nandana,
āmāra parāṇa dhana

śrī⋅rūpa mañjarī, prabhṛtira sama,
jugala sevāya āśa
avaśya serūpa, sevā pāba āmi,
parākāṣṭhā suviśvāsa

kabe vā e dāsī, saṁsiddhi labhibe,
rādhā⋅kuṇḍe vāsa kari’
rādhā⋅kṛṣṇa⋅sevā, satata karibe,
pūrva smṛti parihari’
en_translation:
Her complexion is like lightning, her garments are like a constellation of stars, and her name is Kamala Mañjarī. She is an abode of self-bliss and happiness.

Camphor is in her service; among Lalitā's associates, Rādhā is the leader of the group. The Lord of my mistress is Śrī⋅nanda⋅nandana; He is the treasure of my life.

Like Śrī Rūpa Mañjarī and other friends, my hope is for the service of the Divine Couple. Certainly I will attain such service; this is my supreme and firm faith.

When will this maidservant attain spiritual perfection, residing in Rādhā-kuṇḍa? Constantly engaged in the service of Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa, abandoning all previous memories?
